# 2026-07-22 — QC Pass: the Dated Layer, 2026-07-16 → 07-22, Pre-Push
**Reviewer:** Claude (Fable 5), same session as the 07-22 external review (companion to it; this document is the close theoretical read of the unpushed dated documents that Will requested as the push gate).
**Method:** Every dated document from 07-16 through 07-22 was read in full — the battery arc, OT1 records, campaign notes, consolidation entries, and assessments by me directly; the thirteen 07-19/07-20 technical notes via a dedicated reader instructed to re-derive load-bearing identities rather than trust [V] tags, with its findings adopted here as my own only where the quoted evidence supports them. Verdicts are mine. Scripts and their ledgers are treated as attested (per Will, 07-22) and appear only in the footnote at §5.

**Verdict scale.** CLEAN = push as a dated record as-is. STAMP = push after a short status/supersession stamp (paste-ready texts in §2). HOLD = fix before any public push.

---

*CLEAN\* = clean to push, with the noted caveat carried into the record's next revision or its citations.*

Companion housekeeping (not documents, but push-relevant): the Transcripts folder's 3B1B file is the same SOURCE_/TRANSCRIPT_ misfiling (P3's original case) — stub it the same way; External Reference Papers (20 items, all third-party arXiv material, incl. a triplicated 2412.00879) should be indexed (EXTERNAL_REFS.md), never mirrored; the second completeness-reduction copy and the doubled 07-20 verify scripts dedupe per P6.

## 2. Paste-ready stamps

**S1 — gaussian_witness_capacity_audit ActiveV1, at the head of the closure section:**
> **[Status stamp, 2026-07-22.]** The closure below ("no presumption, no free constant") is gauge-dependent per this record's own Delta1 (same day), §E1, which grades the free-constant claim erratum-grade under the CNG_B gauge. Read the closure jointly with Delta1's gauge table. Text retained unmodified.

**S2 — gaussian_witness_Delta1, at §S3/S4:**
> **[Status stamp, 2026-07-22.]** The "rung law" framing below is superseded by 2026-07-19 seam_toll §C.0: read "self-reference wiring." Terminology only; the identities stand.

**S3 — two_ledgers_legendre ActiveV1, in §1.2 at the GMC row:**
> **[Clarification, 2026-07-22.]** "Linear spend" in this row is priced on cumulative increment variance (Delta1 S4's rule), not on c_m = −Δln u as defined above — under the latter, identical adjacent budgets give zero spend. The trichotomy stands under the S4 pricing.

**S4 — hbar_c_conjugate_pairs, at the header:**
> **[Verification-basis note, 2026-07-22.]** The referenced /tmp/audit5.py was not archived and the promised outputs were not pasted; the [V] marks below are testimony pending script reconstruction (the identities are one-line dimensional checks and re-derive freely).

**S5 — sigma_completion DraftV1, at the headline:**
> **[Status stamp, 2026-07-22.]** "Σ-form (new)" is deflated by 2026-07-20 polar_identification [I9]: the Σ-diagonal mechanism is the engine of Arıkan's polar coding. Novelty residue (two-chirality, cross-face, λ-tower) per that record's own accounting. Text retained unmodified.

**S6 — ot1_delta EXHIBITION_GRADED ActiveV2:** the three amendments listed in §1's verdict row, with replacement resolution language per the companion review §1.3 — these are content amendments, not stamps, and gate the countersign.

**S7 — countersign-state resolution:** either the 07-19 odds-gauge countersign happened (re-stamp the parent to ActiveV1) or it didn't (fix Delta1's "post-countersign" header). Six 07-19/07-20 documents remain DraftV1 pending countersign; push with those labels prominent, or record outcomes first.

## 3. Cross-cutting observations

1. **The week's signature is forward-resolved-but-never-back-stamped.** No settled canon is contradicted; every real tension (gauge closure vs Delta1, rung law vs C.0, Σ-novelty vs polar) was resolved *forward* within a day and never propagated backward. The stamps in §2 are the entire distance between "unexamined drafts" and a publishable layer — the layer is much closer to push-ready than the volume suggested.
2. **[V] vs [T] hygiene is mostly good**, with residual classical facts under bare [V] (the linear-Gaussian MMSE line in the capacity audit; standing-wave identities in seam_toll) — cheap to retag, not blocking.
3. **The one outright unresolved error found in fourteen technical notes** is the two_ledgers GMC row (S3). One clarifying line.
4. **Add to P7:** the V2 Central Reference's §2.2 critical-damping/Butterworth sentence (self-refuting as written), §2.1's residual derivation-language ("emerges… not as an external input," contradicted four lines later), and Tier 4.2's 30–40% vs ~10%/p≈0.1 discrepancy — none tracked in any patch list to date.
5. **Missing verification bases:** chain_audit.py, audit2.py, audit5.py — the only [V]-bearing scripts of the layer not in Drive. Archive or annotate (S4 pattern).

## 4. Push manifest (Drive → repo), for when the gate opens

**20_records/2026-07/**: all dated records and notes in §1's table except the assessments row and holds — 27 files, stamps applied per §2, DraftV1 labels intact. **30_assessments/external/**: 07-20 external_review ActiveV3 (with errata), 07-21 liu_external_check_closure, and — if countersigned in — the two 07-22 review documents (this one and the companion), as offered drafts. **50_code/ot1/full_width_instrument/**: the 27-file instrument bundle (already staged locally from Drive, byte-exact; includes the pre-registered JSON). **50_code/verify/**: the deduped verify/audit series (17 files, staged). **90_archive/transcripts/**: the 2025-12-13 openrouter export. **Excluded from public push:** fullRSB full text and 3B1B full text (SOURCE_ stubs + links instead); External Reference Papers (EXTERNAL_REFS.md index instead); host CSVs and .npy binaries (Drive-indexed per the DATA_MANIFEST pattern). **Separate curation commits** (each independently revertible, for countersign): stamps S1–S5; P6 mechanical fixes (dedupe canon errata twin, 07-03 header, verify-script dedupes); P1 README rebilling of 2-2-26; P2 spine-trace Liu stamp (two-sided, per E4).

## 5. Scripts footnote (attested; two findings passed along)

Per Will's direction the scripts are attested by their ledgers and got no further QC effort. Two findings from the parallel re-execution that completed before that direction arrived, recorded because they are QC-relevant and already paid for: **(a)** the pre-registered prediction JSON's md5 re-computes to exactly the stamped 7fdd69c80d6fa0c35ff45b56d6a2d0e2, and 17 verify/audit scripts re-ran green in a fresh environment (~200 PASS lines, all deliberate refutation-lines behaving as labeled); **(b)** two check-construction defects worth a line in the ledger: verify_cross_budget A12b tests the wrong residual (the claim is true; the check as coded can never see it), and A14's neutral-fixed-point tolerance (1e-4 at 400 iterations of a ~2/n-converging map) is unattainable in any environment — if a PASS was ever logged for A14, that log is worth a puzzled look. Neither touches a filed record's mathematics.

---

*Offered, not self-filed. With §2's stamps applied and the two HOLD items stubbed, my judgment is that the dated layer is push-ready: nothing in it required retraction, one line required correction, and the rest required only that the maps say where the ledger moved — which is the archive's own standard.*
